title: Trigonometry, Vectors, and Periodic Systems · Model Repair Bay
summary: Plan bearings, slopes, cyclic demand, and resultant forces. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a navigation and periodic-demand model and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use radians and the unit circle to define trigonometric functions and periodicity.
Apply right-triangle ratios with diagrams, units, and angle conditions.
Choose laws of sines or cosines and detect ambiguous cases.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-intermediate cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

# Add one tested model increment per activity.

steps.title: Recalculate the repaired case
steps.instruction: Perpendicular components are 9 units and 12 units. Calculate the resultant magnitude. Use the result to decide Sinusoidal Fit for a navigation and periodic-demand model.
steps.why: The Sinusoidal Fit result for a navigation and periodic-demand model is incomplete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.

content.text: Use √(9² + 12²) and check that the result exceeds either component. Reconcile the result with this competency evidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Fit amplitude, period, phase, and midline to a periodic dataset.

content.title: Before calculating
content.text: Before calculating Sinusoidal Fit, predict the sign and approximate magnitude for a navigation and periodic-demand model.
